sanskrit: आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन । मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: हे अर्जुन  ब्रह्मलोकतक सभी लोक पुनरावर्ती है परन्तु हे कौन्तेय  मुझे प्राप्त होनेपर पुनर्जन्म नहीं होता ॥
englishTranslation: All worlds up to Brahma's realm return again, Arjuna. But having reached me, O son of Kunti, there is no rebirth.
essence: Even the highest attainments return, but union with Krishna does not.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Even the highest realms are temporary and lead back into rebirth. Only reaching Krishna ends that cycle completely.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing that the deepest security is beyond every passing world.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield at Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen before war. Krishna has already spoken of remembrance and liberation, and now he sharpens the point: even the highest heavens are not final. Only reaching him ends the cycle of return.
modernRelevance: You finally get what you wanted, and the relief lasts a week. Then the old restlessness comes back. This verse points to a security that does not wear out.
translationLiteral: आब्रह्मभुवनात् (up to the world of Brahma) / लोकाः (worlds) / पुनरावर्तिनः (returning again) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / माम् (me) / उपेत्य (having reached) / तु (but) / कौन्तेय (son of Kunti) / पुनर्जन्म (rebirth) / न (not) / विद्यते (exists)
